title: Add NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler for network isolation
description:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** NetworkPolicy Sub-Reconciler

Add an optional `spec.networkPolicy` field to the Keystone CRD that, when set, creates Kubernetes `NetworkPolicy` resources restricting traffic to and from Keystone API pods. Ingress is limited to port 5000 from user-defined namespace/pod selectors. Egress is auto-derived from existing spec fields (`spec.database.ClusterRef` → MariaDB:3306, `spec.cache.ClusterRef` → Memcached:11211) plus implicit DNS (UDP+TCP 53), with support for additional user-defined egress rules via `additionalEgress`. This implements Zero-Trust/Defense-in-Depth for the Identity Service.

The sub-reconciler implements the optional sub-resource lifecycle with three paths:
1. **spec.networkPolicy nil + no existing NetworkPolicy** → set `NetworkPolicyReady=True` reason `NetworkPolicyNotRequired`, return early
2. **spec.networkPolicy nil + existing NetworkPolicy found** → explicit `client.Delete`, set `NetworkPolicyReady=True` reason `NetworkPolicyNotRequired`
3. **spec.networkPolicy set** → build NetworkPolicy with auto-derived egress + user ingress/additional egress → create-or-update with `SetControllerReference`, set `NetworkPolicyReady=True`

Design decisions: OpenBao egress is excluded (ESO controller handles PushSecret communication, not Keystone pods). Brownfield auto-derivation is excluded (external host:port targets are too varied; users configure via `additionalEgress`).

Includes `NetworkPolicySpec` struct, CEL `XValidation` requiring at least one ingress source, RBAC markers for `networking.k8s.io`, `Owns(&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{})` watch, and unit tests.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go

stories.title: Platform engineer enables NetworkPolicy for Zero-Trust pod isolation
stories.want: to set spec.networkPolicy on the Keystone CR to restrict pod traffic
stories.so_that: Keystone API pods communicate only with authorized sources and backends, implementing defense-in-depth for the identity service
criteria: A NetworkPolicy named '{keystoneName}-api' is created in the Keystone namespace with ownerReference to the Keystone CR
Ingress rules restrict traffic to TCP port 5000 from user-specified namespace/pod selectors
Egress auto-derives MariaDB (TCP 3306) from database.ClusterRef and Memcached (TCP 11211) from cache.ClusterRef
DNS egress (UDP+TCP 53 to kube-system) is always included
NetworkPolicyReady condition is set to True with reason NetworkPolicyReady and correct ObservedGeneration
NetworkPolicy ObjectMeta has commonLabels (app.kubernetes.io/name, instance, managed-by)

stories.title: Platform engineer configures ingress sources for Keystone API access
stories.want: to specify which namespaces and pods can reach Keystone API on port 5000
stories.so_that: only authorized service consumers (Nova, Glance, Horizon, ingress controllers) can access the identity service
criteria: Each ingress source has a required namespaceSelector (map[string]string label selector)
Each ingress source has an optional podSelector (map[string]string label selector)
CEL XValidation rejects spec.networkPolicy with zero ingress sources
All specified sources appear as From peers in a single NetworkPolicy ingress rule on TCP 5000

stories.title: Platform engineer disables NetworkPolicy by removing the field
stories.want: to remove spec.networkPolicy (set to nil) to disable network isolation
stories.so_that: traffic flows unrestricted when policy enforcement is not needed or during troubleshooting
criteria: Existing NetworkPolicy resource is deleted from the namespace (client.Delete with IgnoreNotFound)
NetworkPolicyReady condition is set to True with reason NetworkPolicyNotRequired
No error occurs when spec.networkPolicy is nil and no NetworkPolicy previously existed
Reconciler returns ctrl.Result{} immediately after setting the condition

stories.title: Platform engineer adds custom egress for brownfield or external services
stories.want: to add additionalEgress rules for targets not covered by auto-derivation (brownfield DB, OpenBao plugins, external APIs)
stories.so_that: Keystone pods can reach all required backends even in mixed managed/brownfield environments
criteria: additionalEgress accepts raw networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule type for maximum flexibility
Additional rules are appended after auto-derived egress rules (DNS, MariaDB, Memcached)
Auto-derived rules remain present alongside additional rules
No auto-derived egress for database/cache when only brownfield fields are set (ClusterRef nil)

stories.title: Operator observes NetworkPolicy status through conditions
stories.want: to check the NetworkPolicyReady condition in the Keystone CR status
stories.so_that: I can verify network isolation state, monitor reconciliation health, and troubleshoot issues
criteria: NetworkPolicyReady appears in status.conditions with correct Type, Status, Reason, and Message
ObservedGeneration matches the current Keystone CR generation
Ready aggregate condition includes NetworkPolicyReady in subConditionTypes evaluation
Reason differentiates active policy (NetworkPolicyReady) from disabled (NetworkPolicyNotRequired)

requirements.description: The operator SHALL create a NetworkPolicy targeting Keystone API pods via selectorLabels when spec.networkPolicy is set
requirements.rationale: The NetworkPolicy resource is the core mechanism for enforcing Zero-Trust network isolation on Keystone API pods

scenarios.name: NetworkPolicy created with correct pod selector and metadata
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set with at least one ingress source
scenarios.then: a NetworkPolicy named '{keystoneName}-api' is created with PodSelector matching selectorLabels (app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance={name})
and_then: PolicyTypes include both Ingress and Egress
NetworkPolicy has controller ownerReference to the Keystone CR
commonLabels are set on ObjectMeta

scenarios.name: NetworkPolicy updated when spec changes
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is modified (e.g., ingress source added or egress rule changed)
scenarios.then: the existing NetworkPolicy is updated to reflect the new desired spec
and_then: owner reference and labels are preserved/reconciled
update only issued when spec or metadata actually changed

scenarios.name: NetworkPolicy re-created if deleted externally
scenarios.when: an owned NetworkPolicy is deleted out-of-band and reconciliation triggers via Owns watch
scenarios.then: a new NetworkPolicy is created matching the desired spec with SetControllerReference
and_then: NetworkPolicyReady condition is set to True

requirements.description: The operator SHALL auto-derive MariaDB TCP 3306 egress from spec.database.ClusterRef when set
requirements.rationale: Managed MariaDB uses an in-cluster Service discoverable from ClusterRef; auto-deriving egress eliminates manual configuration for the most common deployment mode

scenarios.name: MariaDB egress auto-derived from ClusterRef
scenarios.when: spec.database.ClusterRef is set (managed mode) and spec.networkPolicy is set
scenarios.then: an egress rule for TCP port 3306 is included in the NetworkPolicy egress list
and_then: rule targets pods/namespace matching the MariaDB service

scenarios.name: No MariaDB egress when brownfield only
scenarios.when: spec.database.Host is set with no ClusterRef (brownfield mode)
scenarios.then: no auto-derived MariaDB egress rule is added
and_then: user must add explicit rules via additionalEgress for brownfield database access

requirements.description: The operator SHALL auto-derive Memcached TCP 11211 egress from spec.cache.ClusterRef when set
requirements.rationale: Managed Memcached uses an in-cluster headless Service discoverable from ClusterRef; auto-deriving egress eliminates manual configuration for the common case

scenarios.name: Memcached egress auto-derived from ClusterRef
scenarios.when: spec.cache.ClusterRef is set (managed mode) and spec.networkPolicy is set
scenarios.then: an egress rule for TCP port 11211 is included in the NetworkPolicy egress list
and_then: rule targets pods/namespace matching the Memcached service

scenarios.name: No Memcached egress when brownfield only
scenarios.when: spec.cache.Servers is set with no ClusterRef (brownfield mode)
scenarios.then: no auto-derived Memcached egress rule is added
and_then: user must add explicit rules via additionalEgress for brownfield cache access

requirements.description: The operator SHALL always include DNS egress (UDP+TCP port 53 to kube-system) in the NetworkPolicy
requirements.rationale: DNS resolution is required for all pod communication; without it service discovery breaks and all egress rules targeting services by name would silently fail

scenarios.name: DNS egress present alongside auto-derived egress
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set and database/cache ClusterRef are both set
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy includes an egress rule for UDP port 53 and TCP port 53 targeting kube-system namespace
and_then: DNS egress is the first egress rule in the list

scenarios.name: DNS egress present even without auto-derived egress
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set but only brownfield database/cache (no ClusterRef set)
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy still includes the DNS egress rule
and_then: DNS is the only auto-derived rule; user provides rest via additionalEgress

scenarios.name: DNS rule includes both UDP and TCP protocols
scenarios.when: any NetworkPolicy is built
scenarios.then: the DNS egress rule has two ports: UDP 53 and TCP 53
and_then: both protocols are required because DNS-over-TCP is used for responses exceeding 512 bytes

requirements.description: The operator SHALL restrict ingress to TCP port 5000 from user-defined namespace/pod selectors specified in spec.networkPolicy.ingress
requirements.rationale: Keystone API listens on port 5000; restricting ingress to explicitly specified sources enforces Zero-Trust for the most security-sensitive OpenStack component

scenarios.name: Ingress from namespace selector only
scenarios.when: an ingress source specifies only namespaceSelector (podSelector is nil/empty)
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy allows ingress on TCP 5000 from any pod in namespaces matching the label selector

scenarios.name: Ingress from namespace + pod selector
scenarios.when: an ingress source specifies both namespaceSelector and podSelector
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy allows ingress on TCP 5000 only from pods matching both selectors (AND logic)

scenarios.name: Multiple ingress sources produce multiple From peers
scenarios.when: two or more ingress sources are specified
scenarios.then: all sources appear as separate From peers in a single NetworkPolicy ingress rule on TCP 5000
and_then: traffic from any matching source is allowed (OR logic across peers)

requirements.description: The operator SHALL append user-defined additionalEgress rules to the NetworkPolicy after auto-derived rules
requirements.rationale: Brownfield targets, external services, and custom plugins (e.g., OpenBao) may need egress not auto-derivable from the CRD spec; additionalEgress provides escape-hatch flexibility

scenarios.name: Additional egress rules appended after auto-derived rules
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy.additionalEgress contains one or more networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule entries
scenarios.then: all additional egress rules are appended to the NetworkPolicy egress list after DNS and ClusterRef-derived rules
and_then: auto-derived rules (DNS, MariaDB, Memcached) are not removed or reordered

scenarios.name: No additional egress when field is empty or nil
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy.additionalEgress is nil or an empty slice
scenarios.then: only auto-derived egress rules appear in the NetworkPolicy

requirements.description: The operator SHALL implement the three-path nil-check lifecycle for the spec.networkPolicy optional pointer field
requirements.rationale: Consistent lifecycle management (matching CC-0038 HPA pattern) ensures platform engineers can enable/disable network isolation without leftover resources or stale conditions

scenarios.name: Path 1: spec.networkPolicy nil + no existing NetworkPolicy
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is nil and no NetworkPolicy named '{name}-api' exists in the namespace
scenarios.then: NetworkPolicyReady condition is set to True with reason NetworkPolicyNotRequired and message 'Network policy is not configured'
and_then: delete is a no-op (client.IgnoreNotFound)
reconciler returns ctrl.Result{} with nil error

scenarios.name: Path 2: spec.networkPolicy nil + existing NetworkPolicy found
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is nil but a NetworkPolicy named '{name}-api' from a previous reconciliation exists
scenarios.then: the existing NetworkPolicy is deleted via client.Delete (IgnoreNotFound) and NetworkPolicyReady is set to True/NetworkPolicyNotRequired
and_then: condition message is 'Network policy is not configured'

scenarios.name: Path 3: spec.networkPolicy set
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set with valid ingress sources
scenarios.then: NetworkPolicy is created or updated via ensureNetworkPolicy with SetControllerReference, and NetworkPolicyReady is set to True/NetworkPolicyReady
and_then: condition message is 'NetworkPolicy is configured'

requirements.description: The CRD SHALL enforce CEL XValidation requiring at least one ingress source when spec.networkPolicy is set
requirements.rationale: A NetworkPolicy with no ingress sources blocks ALL traffic to Keystone API port 5000, causing an immediate service outage — CEL validation prevents this at admission time

scenarios.name: Validation passes with one or more ingress sources
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set with at least one item in the ingress array
scenarios.then: the CR passes CEL validation and is accepted by the API server

scenarios.name: Validation rejects empty ingress array
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set but the ingress array is empty
scenarios.then: the API server rejects the CR with a validation error message 'at least one ingress source must be specified'

requirements.description: The operator SHALL integrate reconcileNetworkPolicy into the controller with RBAC, Owns watch, condition aggregation, and correct reconcile chain position
requirements.rationale: Controller integration ensures NetworkPolicy reconciliation runs as part of the main loop with proper Kubernetes permissions and event-driven re-reconciliation on NetworkPolicy changes

scenarios.name: NetworkPolicyReady included in subConditionTypes aggregation
scenarios.when: setReadyCondition evaluates all sub-conditions for the aggregate Ready status
scenarios.then: NetworkPolicyReady is checked as part of the aggregation — Ready is False if NetworkPolicyReady is missing or not True

scenarios.name: Owns watch triggers reconcile on NetworkPolicy changes
scenarios.when: a NetworkPolicy with controller ownerReference to a Keystone CR is modified or deleted externally
scenarios.then: the controller re-reconciles the owning Keystone CR
and_then: NetworkPolicy is corrected to match desired state

scenarios.name: RBAC marker grants networking.k8s.io permissions
scenarios.when: the controller attempts CRUD operations on NetworkPolicy resources
scenarios.then: the RBAC marker +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=networking.k8s.io,resources=networkpolicies,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete is present

scenarios.name: reconcileNetworkPolicy called after reconcileHPA and before reconcileBootstrap
scenarios.when: the main Reconcile loop runs all sub-reconcilers
scenarios.then: reconcileNetworkPolicy is called in the chain after reconcileHPA and before reconcileBootstrap
and_then: early return on error or requeue, consistent with other sub-reconcilers

requirements.description: The operator SHALL propagate errors from NetworkPolicy ensure and delete operations with wrapped context
requirements.rationale: Error propagation ensures the reconciler reports failures correctly, requeues with backoff, and provides actionable error messages for debugging

scenarios.name: Ensure error wrapped and propagated
scenarios.when: creating or updating the NetworkPolicy fails (e.g., API server error, RBAC denial)
scenarios.then: the error is wrapped with fmt.Errorf('ensuring NetworkPolicy: %w', err) and returned from reconcileNetworkPolicy
and_then: the main reconciler requeues with exponential backoff via updateStatus

scenarios.name: Delete error wrapped and propagated (excluding NotFound)
scenarios.when: deleting the NetworkPolicy fails with an error other than NotFound
scenarios.then: the error is wrapped with fmt.Errorf('deleting NetworkPolicy: %w', err) and returned
and_then: NotFound errors are silently ignored via client.IgnoreNotFound

tasks.title: Add NetworkPolicySpec and NetworkPolicyIngressSource types with CEL validation and optional pointer field to keystone_types.go (CC-0039, REQ-001, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go: (1) Add import for networkingv1 'k8s.io/api/networking/v1'. (2) Add NetworkPolicyIngressSource struct with NamespaceSelector map[string]string (required) and PodSelector map[string]string (optional). (3) Add NetworkPolicySpec struct with Ingress []NetworkPolicyIngressSource (required) and AdditionalEgress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule (optional). (4) Add CEL XValidation on NetworkPolicySpec: rule='size(self.ingress) > 0', message='at least one ingress source must be specified'. (5) Add optional pointer field NetworkPolicy *NetworkPolicySpec on KeystoneSpec (after Autoscaling, before ExtraConfig) with +optional marker and json tag 'networkPolicy,omitempty'. Follow existing pattern from Autoscaling *AutoscalingSpec at line 89. Run 'go vet ./...' from operators/keystone/ to verify compilation.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileNetworkPolicy sub-reconciler with buildNetworkPolicy and inline ensure/delete helpers in reconcile_networkpolicy.go (CC-0039, REQ-001 through REQ-007, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go. Implement: (1) reconcileNetworkPolicy(ctx, *Keystone) (ctrl.Result, error) — three-path lifecycle: if spec.networkPolicy nil → deleteNetworkPolicy + set NetworkPolicyReady=True/NetworkPolicyNotRequired; if set → buildNetworkPolicy + ensureNetworkPolicy + set NetworkPolicyReady=True/NetworkPolicyReady. (2) buildNetworkPolicy(*Keystone) *networkingv1.NetworkPolicy — constructs NetworkPolicy with: name=apiResourceName(), labels=commonLabels(), podSelector=selectorLabels(), policyTypes=[Ingress,Egress], ingress=[TCP 5000 from user sources], egress=[DNS always + MariaDB TCP 3306 if database.ClusterRef set + Memcached TCP 11211 if cache.ClusterRef set + additionalEgress]. (3) ensureNetworkPolicy(ctx, client, scheme, owner, *NetworkPolicy) error — get existing, create if not found (SetControllerReference), update if exists (snapshot-compare pattern from EnsureHPA). (4) deleteNetworkPolicy(ctx, client, namespace, name) error — client.Delete with IgnoreNotFound. Follow CC-0038 reconcile_hpa.go pattern exactly. Use SPDX license header. Feature comment: '// Feature: CC-0039'.

tasks.title: Unit tests for buildNetworkPolicy: pod selector, labels, policy types, ingress rules, auto-derived egress, DNS, additionalEgress, brownfield-only (CC-0039, REQ-001 through REQ-006)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go. Follow reconcile_hpa_test.go conventions: (1) npTestScheme() — clientgoscheme + networkingv1 + keystonev1alpha1. (2) npTestKeystone() — minimal fixture with managed database (ClusterRef) and managed cache (ClusterRef). (3) newNPTestReconciler(scheme, ...objs). Tests: TestBuildNetworkPolicy_PodSelector (selectorLabels match), TestBuildNetworkPolicy_Labels (commonLabels), TestBuildNetworkPolicy_PolicyTypes (Ingress+Egress), TestBuildNetworkPolicy_MariaDBEgressFromClusterRef (TCP 3306 present), TestBuildNetworkPolicy_NoMariaDBEgressWhenBrownfield (no ClusterRef → no rule), TestBuildNetworkPolicy_MemcachedEgressFromClusterRef (TCP 11211 present), TestBuildNetworkPolicy_NoMemcachedEgressWhenBrownfield, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_DNSEgressAlwaysPresent, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_DNSEgressIncludesUDPAndTCP, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_IngressFromNamespaceSelector, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_IngressWithPodSelector, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_MultipleIngressSources, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_AdditionalEgressAppended, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_NoAdditionalEgressWhenEmpty, TestBuildNetworkPolicy_BrownfieldOnlyHasDNSEgressOnly. Use NewGomegaWithT(t) assertions. SPDX header. Feature: CC-0039.

tasks.title: Unit tests for reconcileNetworkPolicy lifecycle: create, update, nil paths, ObservedGeneration, error propagation (CC-0039, REQ-007, REQ-010)
tasks.description: In the same test file (reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go), add lifecycle tests following reconcile_hpa_test.go patterns: TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_PolicySet_CreatesNetworkPolicy (verify resource created, ownerRef set, condition=True/NetworkPolicyReady), TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_PolicySet_UpdatesExisting (create first, modify spec, re-reconcile, verify updated), TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_PolicyNil_NoExisting_SetsNotRequired (nil spec, no pre-existing NP, condition=True/NetworkPolicyNotRequired), TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_PolicyNil_ExistingDeleted (pre-create NP, nil spec, verify deleted and condition set), TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_ConditionObservedGeneration (test both paths with specific generation values), TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_EnsureError_Propagated (interceptor.Funcs on Create for *networkingv1.NetworkPolicy, verify error wrapping), TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_DeleteError_Propagated (interceptor.Funcs on Delete, verify error wrapping). Use fake.NewClientBuilder().WithInterceptorFuncs() for error injection.

tasks.title: Integrate reconcileNetworkPolicy into keystone_controller.go: condition type, RBAC marker, Owns watch, reconcile chain (CC-0039, REQ-009)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go: (1) Add 'NetworkPolicyReady' to subConditionTypes slice (after 'HPAReady', before 'BootstrapReady'). (2) Add import: networkingv1 'k8s.io/api/networking/v1'. (3) Add RBAC marker: '// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=networking.k8s.io,resources=networkpolicies,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete' (after the autoscaling RBAC marker). (4) Add Owns(&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{}) in SetupWithManager after Owns(&autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler{}). (5) Add reconcileNetworkPolicy call in the Reconcile method after reconcileHPA and before reconcileBootstrap: 'if result, err := r.reconcileNetworkPolicy(ctx, &keystone); !result.IsZero() || err != nil { return r.updateStatus(ctx, &keystone, result, err) }'. Run 'go vet ./...' and 'go build ./...' from operators/keystone/ to verify.

review_criteria: All 10 requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-010) have corresponding passing unit tests covering happy path, error cases, and edge cases

NetworkPolicy auto-derives egress correctly: MariaDB TCP 3306 only when database.ClusterRef is set, Memcached TCP 11211 only when cache.ClusterRef is set, no auto-derived rules for brownfield-only configs
DNS egress (UDP+TCP 53 to kube-system) is always present in every generated NetworkPolicy regardless of other configuration
Three-path nil-check lifecycle matches CC-0038 HPA pattern exactly: nil+absent → NotRequired, nil+exists → delete+NotRequired, set → ensure+Ready
CEL XValidation marker on NetworkPolicySpec struct enforces size(self.ingress) > 0

Controller integration correct: NetworkPolicyReady in subConditionTypes, RBAC marker for networking.k8s.io/networkpolicies, Owns(&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{}) watch, call positioned after reconcileHPA and before reconcileBootstrap
SetControllerReference called on both create and update paths in ensureNetworkPolicy for owner-based garbage collection
Error wrapping follows project convention: fmt.Errorf('verb context: %w', err) with descriptive action prefix
go vet and golangci-lint pass without errors on all modified/created files
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ keystone_types.go                   │
│  NetworkPolicySpec                  │
│  ├─ Ingress []IngressSource         │
│  └─ AdditionalEgress []EgressRule   │
│  KeystoneSpec.NetworkPolicy *NPS    │
└───────────────┬─────────────────────┘
                │ imports
                ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ reconcile_networkpolicy.go          │
│  reconcileNetworkPolicy()           │
│  ├─ nil path: delete + NotRequired  │
│  └─ set path: build + ensure +Ready │
│  buildNetworkPolicy()               │
│  ├─ ingress: TCP 5000 + user srcs   │
│  ├─ egress: DNS (always)            │
│  ├─ egress: MariaDB (ClusterRef)    │
│  ├─ egress: Memcached (ClusterRef)  │
│  └─ egress: additionalEgress        │
│  ensureNetworkPolicy()              │
│  deleteNetworkPolicy()              │
└───────────────┬─────────────────────┘
                │ called by
                ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ keystone_controller.go              │
│  subConditionTypes += NP Ready      │
│  RBAC: networking.k8s.io            │
│  Owns(NetworkPolicy)                │
│  Reconcile() chain:                 │
│    ...→ reconcileHPA                │
│    ...→ reconcileNetworkPolicy NEW  │
│    ...→ reconcileBootstrap          │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Data Flow

1. User sets `spec.networkPolicy` with ingress sources → Kubernetes API server validates CEL (size(self.ingress)>0) → CR accepted
2. Controller receives reconcile event → calls reconcileNetworkPolicy
3. reconcileNetworkPolicy checks `spec.networkPolicy` pointer:
   - nil → deleteNetworkPolicy (no-op if absent) → set condition NotRequired → return
   - set → buildNetworkPolicy reads spec.database.ClusterRef, spec.cache.ClusterRef, spec.networkPolicy.ingress/additionalEgress → constructs networkingv1.NetworkPolicy → ensureNetworkPolicy (create-or-update) → set condition Ready → return
4. Owns watch on NetworkPolicy triggers re-reconcile if NP modified externally → drift corrected

## Key Decisions

1. **No common helper**: ensureNetworkPolicy/deleteNetworkPolicy are private functions in reconcile_networkpolicy.go (not in internal/common/deployment/) because NetworkPolicy construction is Keystone-specific due to auto-derived rules. Generalize when a second operator needs it.
2. **Inline ensure follows EnsureHPA pattern**: Get existing → if NotFound: SetControllerReference + Create → if exists: snapshot before, reconcile metadata (ownerRefs/labels/annotations), set spec, compare, update if changed.
3. **NetworkPolicy name**: Uses apiResourceName() = '{keystoneName}-api' (same as Deployment, HPA, PDB) for consistent naming.
4. **Egress rule ordering**: DNS first (always), MariaDB second (conditional), Memcached third (conditional), additionalEgress last (user-defined).
5. **No brownfield auto-derivation**: When ClusterRef is nil, no egress is auto-derived for that service. External host:port targets are too varied to safely auto-derive NetworkPolicy selectors.
6. **OpenBao excluded**: Keystone pods never contact OpenBao directly — ESO controller handles PushSecret communication from a separate namespace/deployment.

## Pitfalls

1. **NetworkPolicy import alias**: Use `networkingv1 "k8s.io/api/networking/v1"` consistently (matches existing alias convention: appsv1, corev1, policyv1, autoscalingv2).
2. **intstr.IntOrString for ports**: NetworkPolicy port values use `intstr.IntOrString`, not raw int32. Import `"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr"`.
3. **Protocol pointer**: NetworkPolicy ports require `*corev1.Protocol` (pointer), not `corev1.Protocol`. Use `protocolTCP := corev1.ProtocolTCP; port.Protocol = &protocolTCP`.
4. **Test scheme registration**: Must add `networkingv1.AddToScheme(s)` (using `clientgoscheme.AddToScheme` covers core types but NOT networking/v1 — it must be registered explicitly).
5. **DeepCopy generation**: After adding types to keystone_types.go, `controller-gen` must regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go. NetworkPolicySpec contains a slice of networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule which has its own DeepCopyInto — the generator handles this automatically.

## Deferred Items

1. **Integration test (envtest)**: Add NetworkPolicy verification to internal/controller/integration_test.go. Deferred because: feature scope explicitly says 'unit tests'. Revisit: next integration test sweep.
2. **Common EnsureNetworkPolicy/DeleteNetworkPolicy**: Extract to internal/common/deployment/ when a second operator needs NetworkPolicy management. Deferred because: currently Keystone-specific due to auto-derived rules.
3. **NetworkPolicy for CronJob/Job pods**: Fernet/credential rotation CronJobs and db_sync/bootstrap Jobs have different pod selectors (app.kubernetes.io/component differs). Deferred because: separate concern, not requested.
4. **Brownfield auto-derivation**: Auto-deriving egress from spec.database.Host or spec.cache.Servers would require CIDR-based rules, which are fragile with DNS-resolved addresses. Deferred because: too varied to auto-derive safely; users configure via additionalEgress.
5. **Webhook validation**: CEL XValidation markers suffice for the current validation needs. Deferred because: consistent with existing approach (no webhook-based validation for other optional fields).
